peripherals.name: I2C Scanner
peripherals.description: Scans the I2C bus and reports all responding addresses. Essential first step when connecting I2C devices.
peripherals.esp32_example: Wire.begin(SDA, SCL); for(byte addr=1; addr<127; addr++) { Wire.beginTransmission(addr); if(Wire.endTransmission()==0) Serial.printf("Found: 0x%02X\n", addr); }
peripherals.arduino_example: Same code but Wire.begin() with no pins. Use Serial.begin(9600).
common_pitfalls: SDA/SCL pins not set correctly for ESP32
Pull-up resistors missing (2.2K-10K on both lines)
I2C speed too high for long wires
Multiple devices with same address conflict

peripherals.name: PWM Motor Control
peripherals.description: Controls DC motor speed using PWM. Use motor driver (L298N, TB6612, DRV8833) to handle current. ESP32 has dedicated LEDC PWM with 16 channels.
common_pitfalls: Motor driver needs separate power supply from logic
No flyback diodes on some motor drivers
PWM frequency too low causes motor whine
GPIO can't supply enough current - always use driver

peripherals.name: Servo Motor Control
peripherals.description: Controls servo motors using PWM (50Hz, 1-2ms pulse width). ESP32 LEDC or Arduino Servo library.
common_pitfalls: Servo library on ESP32 uses timer conflicts
Pulse width out of range damages servo
Power supply can't supply servo stall current
Servo draws spikes that reset the MCU

peripherals.name: NeoPixel (WS2812B) LED Strip
peripherals.description: Addressable RGB LED strip. Each LED has IC. 800KHz data rate, very timing sensitive. ESP32 uses RMT peripheral for glitch-free output.
peripherals.esp32_example: FastLED.addLeds<WS2812B, 5, GRB>(leds, NUM_LEDS); fill_solid(leds, NUM_LEDS, CRGB::Red); FastLED.show();
common_pitfalls: No capacitor across +5V/GND on large strips
Data line needs level shifter (5V strip with 3.3V MCU)
Too many LEDs exceed power supply or RAM
Timing too tight for software bit-banging - use RMT

peripherals.name: OLED Display (SSD1306)
peripherals.description: 128x64 monochrome OLED. I2C is 2-wire simple. SPI is faster but needs more pins. u8g2 library supports many displays.
peripherals.esp32_example: U8G2_SSD1306_128X64_NONAME_F_HW_I2C u8g2(U8G2_R0, U8X8_PIN_NONE, 22, 21); u8g2.begin(); u8g2.drawStr(0, 20, "Hello"); u8g2.sendBuffer();
common_pitfalls: I2C address 0x3C vs 0x3D
Display buffer size: 128*64/8 = 1024 bytes
Need to call display() or sendBuffer() after drawing
Font size affects available lines

peripherals.name: SD Card Storage
peripherals.description: Read/write files on SD card. Use SPI mode. Supports FAT16/FAT32. Essential for data logging.
peripherals.esp32_example: SD.begin(5); File f = SD.open("/log.txt", FILE_APPEND); f.print(data); f.close();
common_pitfalls: SD card needs 3.3V signal (not 5V)
SPI speed too high causes read/write errors
Long wires degrade SPI signal quality
Must close files properly or data is lost

peripherals.name: IR Remote (Send/Receive)
peripherals.description: Send or receive IR remote signals. ESP32 can use IRremote library for both TX and RX. NEC protocol most common.
peripherals.esp32_example: IRsend irsend(4); irsend.sendNEC(0x20DF10EF); // TX. IRrecv irrecv(15); irrecv.enableIRIn();
peripherals.arduino_example: Same IRremote library. IRsend irsend(3); irsend.sendSony(0xA90, 12);
common_pitfalls: IR LED needs transistor driver for range
Receiver module needs pull-up on output
NEC vs Sony vs other protocols
Ambient IR light causes false triggers

peripherals.name: Rotary Encoder
peripherals.description: Rotary encoder for menu navigation, volume control. Outputs A and B phase signals. Use interrupts for reliable detection.
peripherals.esp32_example: attachInterrupt(25, readEncoder, CHANGE); attachInterrupt(26, readEncoder, CHANGE);
peripherals.arduino_example: Same interrupt approach. Pin 2 and 3 recommended for Uno (interrupt pins).
common_pitfalls: Contact bounce causes multiple counts per click
Must use interrupts (polling misses pulses)
Encoder type: incremental vs absolute
Mechanical vs optical encoders

peripherals.name: Interrupts (External)
peripherals.description: React to pin state changes instantly. ESP32: any GPIO except strapping pins. Use for: buttons, encoders, sensors, time-critical events.
peripherals.esp32_example: pinMode(25, INPUT_PULLUP); attachInterrupt(25, ISR, FALLING); IRAM_ATTR void ISR() { counter++; }
common_pitfalls: ISRs must be fast - no Serial.print inside
Variables shared with ISR need volatile or atomic
ESP32: ISRs in flash need IRAM_ATTR
Debounce needed for mechanical switches

peripherals.name: FreeRTOS Tasks
peripherals.description: Run multiple tasks concurrently on ESP32's dual-core. Use queues for inter-task communication. Tasks run on specific cores or either.
peripherals.arduino_example: Arduino uses standard FreeRTOS. xTaskCreate(task1, "Task1", 4096, NULL, 1, NULL);
common_pitfalls: Stack size too small causes crash
Priority inversion deadlock
Not using vTaskDelay blocks other tasks
Accessing shared resources without mutex

peripherals.name: Deep Sleep + Wake Sources
peripherals.description: Power saving mode. ESP32 RTC domain stays on. Wake sources: timer, touch, ext0, ext1, GPIO, ULP. Current ~10uA in deep sleep.
common_pitfalls: GPIO state changes during sleep can wake unexpectedly
RTC GPIO only (0,2,4,12-15,25-27,32-33) for ext wake
Values lost in regular RAM during sleep
Serial output doesn't work after deep sleep

peripherals.name: OTA (Over-The-Air) Updates
peripherals.description: Update firmware wirelessly without physical access. ESP32 uses ESP OTA or ArduinoOTA library. HTTPS preferred for security.
peripherals.esp32_example: ArduinoOTA.begin(); // in setup. ArduinoOTA.handle(); // in loop. Add MDNS for easy discovery.
peripherals.arduino_example: Same ArduinoOTA library. Set password, hostname, port 3232.
common_pitfalls: OTA partition size must be configured in platformio.ini
Power interruption during flash corrupts firmware
No password = security risk
MDNS may not resolve on all networks

peripherals.name: ESP-NOW (Peer-to-Peer)
peripherals.description: Fast peer-to-peer communication between ESP32 boards without WiFi router. Low latency ~3ms. Good for remote control, sensor networks.
peripherals.esp32_example: esp_now_init(); esp_now_add_peer(mac, ESP_NOW_ROLE_COMBO, 0, NULL, 0); esp_now_send(mac, data, len);
peripherals.arduino_example: Espnow library wrapper for simpler API.
common_pitfalls: Pairing required before first communication
MAC address of peer needed
Encrypted vs unencrypted pairing
Max 10 peers per device in default config
